diagrams.title: Bayes' Rule Components
diagrams.description: Shows the relationship between prior probability, likelihood, evidence, and posterior probability
elements: P(H) - Prior: Initial belief before evidence
P(E) - Evidence: Probability of observing evidence
P(H|E) - Posterior: Updated belief after evidence
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how Bayes' Rule updates beliefs with new evidence

diagrams.title: Expert System Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows the main components and their interactions in an Expert System
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the structure and function of Expert System components

diagrams.title: Bayesian Network Example
diagrams.description: A simple Bayesian Network showing conditional dependencies for a medical scenario
elements: Cloudy node with arrows to Sprinkler and Rain
Sprinkler and Rain both point to WetGrass
Each node has conditional probability table
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how Bayesian Networks represent conditional dependencies graphically
